findings.title: Safe preselection for morning batch logging
findings.current_behavior: Batch logging shows a review list and lets the user deselect or skip individual doses before confirming.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Preselect only doses that are fully loggable: valid inventory, valid route-aware site, and no missing protocol data. Doses with 0 vials, no valid site, or ambiguous state remain visible but unchecked with a concise warning and a one-tap action to skip or resolve.
findings.user_benefit: Power Users can complete the 7am routine quickly without accidentally confirming unsafe or incomplete entries. Delegated Participants get a simpler default path.
findings.cost_reasoning: Uses existing dose, vial, and site data but requires review-list state and per-row validation.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I have scheduled doses and one has 0 vials remaining, when I open Log All Scheduled, then the unavailable dose is shown unchecked with a warning and cannot be batch-confirmed until I explicitly skip or resolve it.
findings.scope_check: In scope: improves smart defaults and inline safety validation within the existing batch logging flow.

findings.title: Reconstitution guardrails at the field level
findings.current_behavior: The calculator displays concentration, syringe units, warnings, and last logged dose after the user enters vial and BAC water values.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Show immediate field-level validation for impossible or risky values, normalize mg/mcg/mL labels visibly, and keep a sticky summary showing concentration, dose volume, and insulin syringe units as inputs change.
findings.user_benefit: Reduces math slips in the highest-risk flow and makes the calculation auditable before saving a vial.
findings.cost_reasoning: Requires careful form state and deterministic validation, but no new domain model.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I enter a BAC water volume below 0.5mL, when the field loses focus or recalculates, then the field shows the yellow guardrail warning inline and the summary continues to show the calculated concentration.
findings.scope_check: In scope: inline validation and better error handling inside the existing reconstitution calculator.

findings.title: Last-used reconstitution volume chip
findings.current_behavior: The user manually enters BAC water volume for each calculation.
findings.proposed_enhancement: When the same compound and vial size were previously reconstituted, show a non-auto-applied chip such as "Use last: 2mL from Apr 18". Tapping fills the field and highlights that it came from prior history.
findings.user_benefit: Speeds repeated vial prep while keeping the user consciously in control of safety-critical math.
findings.cost_reasoning: Reads from existing vial history and writes no new data type.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I previously saved a 5mg BPC-157 vial with 2mL BAC water, when I calculate a new 5mg BPC-157 vial, then I see a "Use last: 2mL" option that fills but does not automatically submit the calculation.
findings.scope_check: In scope: smart default using existing data without recommending a dose or optimizing a protocol.

findings.title: Protocol schedule preview before save
findings.current_behavior: Creating or editing a protocol generates a schedule from compound, dose, start date, and frequency.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Add a compact preview of the next 7 generated dose dates and the assigned user before saving, with inline warnings for missing start date, invalid interval, or inactive managed user.
findings.user_benefit: Power Users can catch schedule mistakes before they affect morning logging or managed users.
findings.cost_reasoning: Reuses the schedule generator but adds preview rendering and validation states.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I choose EOD frequency and a start date, when I review the protocol form, then I see the next 7 generated dose dates before saving.
findings.scope_check: In scope: progressive disclosure and validation inside the existing protocol creation flow.

findings.title: Managed-user adherence batch nudges
findings.current_behavior: The admin panel shows a 7-day adherence percentage for each managed user.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Let the Power User select multiple low-adherence managed users and trigger existing password reset or reminder-setting review actions from the table, without exposing other users' data to participants.
findings.user_benefit: Power Users managing 2-10 people can triage adherence issues faster from the existing admin surface.
findings.cost_reasoning: Adds table selection and bulk UI actions, but uses existing account and reminder flows.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given multiple managed users appear in the adherence table, when I select two rows, then available bulk actions are limited to existing safe account-management actions and are recorded through their existing mutation paths.
findings.scope_check: In scope: batch operation inside existing managed-user administration, not self-serve onboarding.

findings.title: Command-speed catalog and dashboard actions
findings.current_behavior: Users navigate dashboard, catalog search, and batch logging through visible UI controls.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Add keyboard shortcuts for Power Users on desktop: slash focuses catalog search, L opens today's batch log review, Escape closes modals, and Enter confirms only when the focused primary action is enabled.
findings.user_benefit: Gives the solo-developer Power User faster dense workflows without changing mobile-first behavior.
findings.cost_reasoning: Mostly event handling and focus management.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I am on the dashboard using a hardware keyboard, when I press L, then the Log All Scheduled review opens without immediately confirming any dose.
findings.scope_check: In scope: keyboard shortcuts for existing actions.

findings.title: One-glance delegated participant dose card
findings.current_behavior: Managed users see a simplified dashboard with their own schedule and can log individual or batch doses.
findings.proposed_enhancement: For Delegated Participants, collapse the dashboard into a single dominant card when only one dose is due: "Today: 250mcg BPC-157, left abdomen" with Confirm and Skip as the only primary actions.
findings.user_benefit: Matches the low-complexity persona and reduces accidental navigation or configuration attempts.
findings.cost_reasoning: Requires role-aware dashboard layout but relies on existing schedule and log actions.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given a managed user has exactly one dose due today, when they open the dashboard, then the dose appears as the primary card with Confirm and Skip actions and no protocol configuration controls.
findings.scope_check: In scope: progressive disclosure and role-appropriate UX inside existing managed-user and logging stories.

findings.title: Site rotation mini-map
findings.current_behavior: The logging flow shows the suggested injection site and the last 7 sites used for the compound.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Show the last 7 sites as a small tappable body-region map with the suggested site highlighted and unavailable route-incompatible sites dimmed.
findings.user_benefit: Makes rotation instantly understandable under time pressure and helps prevent repeated-site mistakes.
findings.cost_reasoning: Needs a responsive visual component and accessible text equivalent, but no new data.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I am logging a dose with site history, when the site selector is shown, then the suggested site is visually highlighted and the same information is available as text for screen readers.
findings.scope_check: In scope: visual enhancement to the existing injection site rotation flow.

findings.title: Remembered dashboard density
findings.current_behavior: The dashboard shows active cycle, inventory warnings, wellbeing average, adherence, and today's doses.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Let the dashboard remember the user's last selected density mode: compact for Power Users with many protocols, comfortable for Delegated Participants, and expanded when onboarding is incomplete.
findings.user_benefit: Power Users get a denser command surface while simpler users avoid overload.
findings.cost_reasoning: Client preference plus responsive CSS variants.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I choose compact dashboard density, when I return to the dashboard later, then the active cycle, today's doses, and warnings render in compact mode without hiding required safety warnings.
findings.scope_check: In scope: personalization without adding a new product feature.

findings.title: Offline confidence ribbon
findings.current_behavior: Offline dose logs and batch logs are queued and synced when the app is back online.
findings.proposed_enhancement: When offline, show a persistent but compact ribbon such as "Offline: 2 logs saved on this device" with a tap target revealing queued items and last sync time.
findings.user_benefit: Users can trust the 7am workflow even with poor signal and avoid duplicate logging out of uncertainty.
findings.cost_reasoning: Requires queue visibility and sync metadata presentation.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I am offline and log two doses, when I return to the dashboard, then I see that two logs are saved locally and pending sync.
findings.scope_check: In scope: offline UX refinement for existing offline logging behavior.

findings.title: Telegram fallback as first-class recovery
findings.current_behavior: Composed order messages and Telegram deep-links remain visible even if automation is enabled.
findings.proposed_enhancement: If MTProto send fails or times out, keep the order in Draft, show the exact composed message, and place Copy Message and Open Telegram as the recovery actions above retry.
findings.user_benefit: Power Users can complete an order manually without losing the generated message or wondering whether a duplicate was sent.
findings.cost_reasoning: Uses existing fallback artifacts but requires clearer send-state handling.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given Telegram automation fails during Send Order, when the error is shown, then the order remains Draft and the composed message plus Telegram deep-link are immediately available.
findings.scope_check: In scope: better error recovery inside existing Telegram ordering flow.

findings.title: Correlation chart explain-on-hover
findings.current_behavior: A chart shows dose events and outcome ratings over 30 or 90 days, plus average ratings on dosed versus non-dosed days.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Add hover and tap explanations that translate chart points into plain statements, such as the dose, rating, tags, and whether the day counted as dosed or non-dosed for the displayed statistic.
findings.user_benefit: Makes the analytics feel intelligent without adding recommendations or medical interpretation.
findings.cost_reasoning: Requires richer chart tooltips and mobile tap behavior.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I tap a chart point on mobile, when the detail popover opens, then it shows that day's dose events, outcome rating, tags, and whether it is included in dosed-day statistics.
findings.scope_check: In scope: improves an existing visualization without generating dose advice.

findings.title: Accessible safety summaries for charts and maps
findings.current_behavior: Site history and correlation data are primarily visual in the logging and analytics flows.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Provide structured text equivalents: last 7 injection sites as a list, chart data as a table, and dashboard warning badges as screen-reader announced status text.
findings.user_benefit: Users relying on assistive technology get the same safety-critical context as sighted users.
findings.cost_reasoning: Requires semantic markup and alternate representations, not new backend behavior.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I use a screen reader on the correlation timeline, when the chart is focused, then I can access a table containing the same dates, dose events, and outcome ratings.
findings.scope_check: In scope: accessibility improvement for existing interfaces.

findings.title: Thumb-safe payment confirmation gate
findings.current_behavior: The Mark Payment Sent button is enabled only after the user enters wallet address and amount and views a summary screen.
findings.proposed_enhancement: On mobile, chunk the wallet address in a monospaced verification block, keep amount and currency adjacent, and place Mark Payment Sent below a required checkbox that is separated from copy/open-wallet actions.
findings.user_benefit: Reduces fat-finger and visual scanning errors in a non-reversible crypto flow.
findings.cost_reasoning: Mainly layout, typography, and focus-order work.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I am on the payment summary screen on mobile, when I review the wallet address, then it is displayed in readable chunks with the amount and currency visible before the final confirmation control.
findings.scope_check: In scope: defensive mobile UX inside the existing payment confirmation gate.

findings.title: Local duplicate protection for offline batch logs
findings.current_behavior: Offline individual and batch logs are queued and synced once online.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Prevent the same scheduled dose from being queued twice on the same device by replacing the confirm action with a queued state immediately after tap, even before sync completes.
findings.user_benefit: Avoids duplicate logs when the user taps repeatedly during poor connectivity.
findings.cost_reasoning: Requires local idempotency keys tied to scheduled dose identity.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I am offline and confirm a scheduled dose, when I tap the same confirm control again before sync, then the app does not enqueue a duplicate log and shows the dose as queued.
findings.scope_check: In scope: offline error recovery within existing logging behavior.

findings.title: Actionable empty states for no doses and no protocols
findings.current_behavior: The dashboard shows current stack, cycle, inventory, ratings, adherence, and today's doses when data exists.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Use role-aware empty states: Power Users see Create Protocol and Browse Catalog actions; Delegated Participants see "No dose scheduled today" without configuration actions.
findings.user_benefit: Reduces confusion for new users and prevents managed users from encountering controls they cannot use.
findings.cost_reasoning: Conditional UI based on existing role and data state.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given a managed user has no doses scheduled today, when they open the dashboard, then they see a simple no-dose message and no protocol creation action.
findings.scope_check: In scope: empty-state design inside existing dashboard and onboarding flows.

findings.title: Password and email forms with privacy-preserving clarity
findings.current_behavior: Password reset does not reveal account existence, and password/email changes require current-password gates and audit events.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Use consistent helper text and error placement that explains next steps without revealing sensitive state, with autocomplete attributes, visible password length feedback, and focus moving to the first error after submit.
findings.user_benefit: Improves account recovery and settings changes for all users without weakening privacy guarantees.
findings.cost_reasoning: Mostly form semantics, copy, and validation presentation.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I submit an invalid password-change form, when validation fails, then focus moves to the first error and the message does not expose password values or account existence.
findings.scope_check: In scope: accessibility and error handling for existing auth flows.

findings.title: Ordering-disabled navigation fallback
findings.current_behavior: When ordering is disabled, ordering routes return 404 or 403 and ordering navigation links are removed while tracker and reference remain functional.
findings.proposed_enhancement: If a user reaches a disabled ordering deep link, show a sparse access-disabled page with links back to Dashboard and Catalog instead of a generic dead end.
findings.user_benefit: Keeps regulated or disabled deployments understandable without implying ordering is available.
findings.cost_reasoning: Adds a controlled disabled-state route response and navigation fallback.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given DISABLE_ORDERING=true, when I navigate directly to an ordering URL, then I see an ordering-unavailable state with links to Dashboard and Catalog and no ordering actions.
findings.scope_check: In scope: degraded-mode UX for the existing ordering isolation requirement.
